goal: Land the G8 test-discovery backbone — `G8Source` trait + `execute_g8` parallel orchestrator + `merge_g8_test_discoveries` dedup-by-test-path merger — providing the `DEC-0008` §4 dependency-inversion seam for downstream G8-production-wiring WOs (`ConventionG8Source` / `ImportG8Source` / `KgRelationsG8Source` / daemon-startup orchestration). Mirrors the SEXTUPLE-validated G-backbone template (WO-0047 G1, WO-0070 G3, WO-0083 G4, WO-0085 G7) and the master-plan §5.8 lines 561-579 contract: 'Discover ALL relevant tests via ALL methods: 1. Convention-based / 2. Import-based / 3. KG-based — concurrently, then merge.' Single-feature WO; no peer features in feature-list share the G8 thematic invariant (F10 `check_quality` and F14 `testing_pipeline_integration_test` both depend on F09 transitively).
plan_summary: Single-feature G-backbone WO at `crates/ucil-daemon/`. (1) NEW `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs` module with: `G8_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(4_500)` + `G8_DEFAULT_MASTER_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(5_000)` constants matching G7 (`g7.rs:90` precedent); `pub enum TestDiscoveryMethod { Convention, Import, KgRelations }` (3 variants per master-plan §5.8 lines 572-574); `pub struct G8TestCandidate { test_path: PathBuf, source_path: Option<PathBuf>, method: TestDiscoveryMethod, confidence: f64 }`; `pub enum G8SourceStatus { Available, TimedOut, Errored }` mirror of `G7SourceStatus`; `pub struct G8SourceOutput { source_id, method, status, elapsed_ms, candidates, error }`; `pub struct G8Outcome { results, wall_elapsed_ms, master_timed_out }`; `pub struct MergedG8TestCandidate { test_path, methods_found_by, max_confidence, source_paths }`; `pub struct G8Query { changed_files: Vec<PathBuf> }`; `pub trait G8Source: Send + Sync { fn source_id(&self) -> String; fn method(&self) -> TestDiscoveryMethod; async fn execute(&self, query: &G8Query) -> Result<Vec<G8TestCandidate>, String>; }` (DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam — UCIL-owned, not a re-export); `pub async fn execute_g8(...)` parallel orchestrator with per-source `tokio::time::timeout` + master-deadline `tokio::time::timeout` over the join (mirrors `g7.rs:execute_g7` shape verbatim); `pub fn merge_g8_test_discoveries(outcome: &G8Outcome) -> Vec<MergedG8TestCandidate>` pure-deterministic dedup-by-test_path merger that union-aggregates `methods_found_by` across all sources finding the same test_path; private `run_g8_source` helper carrying `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.g8.source", fields(source_id))]`; private `join_all_g8` helper with `#[allow(dead_code)]` comment citing M1 contract per WO-0085 §executor lesson. (2) Frozen test `test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007 + WO-0070/0083/0085 precedent — selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods` resolves uniquely. Three TestG8Source impls (`ConventionTestG8Source`, `ImportTestG8Source`, `KgRelationsTestG8Source`) at `#[cfg(test)]` carve-out per DEC-0008 §4 + WO-0083 `TestG4Source` precedent. (3) `lib.rs` ADD `pub mod g8;` in alphabetical position. Mutation contract: M1 (per-source-timeout bypass in `run_g8_source`) + M2 (merger dedup-key inversion to `(test_path, method)`). Estimated 4-6 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence.
scope_in: 1. **Single-feature G-backbone WO**: P3-W11-F09 has no peer features in the feature-list sharing the G8 thematic invariant — F10 `check_quality` and F14 `testing_pipeline_integration_test` both depend on F09 transitively (cannot bundle pre-F09-landing). The G-backbone template is SEXTUPLE-validated (WO-0047 G1, WO-0070 G3, WO-0083 G4, WO-0085 G7); single-feature WOs are precedent-supported (WO-0079/WO-0080/WO-0087 single-feature) AND substantial enough to warrant a dedicated WO (G7 backbone alone was ~700-1300 LOC per WO-0085 §planner). Cite WO-0086 §planner explicit recommendation: 'P3-W11-F09 G8 test discovery backbone' as eligible single-feature emission.

2. **F09 module file location**: NEW `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs` (file does NOT exist at planner-time — pre-flight `! test -e crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs` returns true). Mirror the existing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs` shape verbatim — same module-header crate-doc style, same constant-naming convention, same `Send + Sync` trait bounds.

3. **F09 frozen-test file location**: extend EXISTING `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` with `pub async fn test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods()` at MODULE ROOT (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`) per DEC-0007 + WO-0070 §verifier + WO-0083 §verifier precedent. The selector `-p ucil-daemon executor::test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods` per `feature-list.json:P3-W11-F09.acceptance_tests[0].selector` pins both the module name (`executor`) AND the function name. The three `TestG8Source` impls live in the same `executor.rs` file under `#[cfg(test)]` adjacent to (or as nested helpers within) the test fn — same shape as `TestG4Source` at `executor.rs:3822` and `TestG3Source` at `executor.rs:~3170`.

4. **G8 module constants** (with full rustdoc citing master-plan §6.1 + §5.8 + a `# Examples` block where non-trivial — per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Docs):
    `pub const G8_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(4_500);` matching `g7.rs:90` (`G7_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE`).
    `pub const G8_DEFAULT_MASTER_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(5_000);` matching the master-plan §6.1 line 606 'Per-group timeout: 5s default'.
    Both constants documented inline citing master-plan §6.1 + §5.8 lines 571-575. Use `tokio::time::timeout` only — never `std::time::*` for timing in async paths per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Async.

5. **G8 public types** (each with full rustdoc + `# Examples` block where non-trivial):
    (a) `pub enum TestDiscoveryMethod { Convention, Import, KgRelations }` — three variants matching the three methods per master-plan §5.8 lines 572-574 verbatim. `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. The `Hash + Eq` bounds are required for `HashSet<TestDiscoveryMethod>` use in the merger's method-union step. Each variant's rustdoc cites the master-plan line where the method is named.
    (b) `pub struct G8TestCandidate { pub test_path: PathBuf, pub source_path: Option<PathBuf>, pub method: TestDiscoveryMethod, pub confidence: f64 }`. `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`. `confidence` ∈ [0.0, 1.0] — rustdoc-asserted invariant; production impls SHOULD clamp via `f.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` but the trait surface does NOT enforce. `source_path` is `None` when the method does NOT identify a single originating source file (e.g., KG-relations may map a test to multiple sources).
    (c) `pub enum G8SourceStatus { Available, TimedOut, Errored }` mirroring `G7SourceStatus` (`g7.rs:218`). `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]`.
    (d) `pub struct G8SourceOutput { pub source_id: String, pub method: TestDiscoveryMethod, pub status: G8SourceStatus, pub elapsed_ms: u64, pub candidates: Vec<G8TestCandidate>, pub error: Option<String> }`. `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. Mirrors `G7SourceOutput` (`g7.rs:230`) + ADDITIONAL `method: TestDiscoveryMethod` field (G8 sources are method-tagged so the merger can union by method).
    (e) `pub struct G8Outcome { pub results: Vec<G8SourceOutput>, pub wall_elapsed_ms: u64, pub master_timed_out: bool }`. Mirrors `G7Outcome` (`g7.rs:255`) verbatim. `results` order matches input `sources` argument order.
    (f) `pub struct G8Query { pub changed_files: Vec<PathBuf> }`. The query payload that production sources consume — list of paths under change for which to discover related tests. Empty `changed_files` is valid (returns empty candidates from each source).
    (g) `pub struct MergedG8TestCandidate { pub test_path: PathBuf, pub methods_found_by: Vec<TestDiscoveryMethod>, pub max_confidence: f64, pub source_paths: Vec<PathBuf> }`. `methods_found_by` is the deduplicated union of `TestDiscoveryMethod` variants that ALL discovered this `test_path` (sorted by enum discriminant order: Convention < Import < KgRelations); `max_confidence` is `max(c.confidence for c in contributing)`; `source_paths` is the deduplicated alphabetical-sort union of all `source_path: Some(_)` values across contributors. NO `#[non_exhaustive]` — stable contract for downstream consumers.
    Each struct MUST derive `Debug, Clone, PartialEq` minimum; `Serialize, Deserialize` for the wire-shape types (status, output, outcome) per WO-0085 §planner precedent.

7. **`execute_g8` orchestrator** (with `# Examples` rustdoc per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Docs):
    `pub async fn execute_g8(query: G8Query, sources: Vec<Box<dyn G8Source + Send + Sync + 'static>>, deadline: Duration) -> G8Outcome`. The function:
    Step 1 — Capture `start = std::time::Instant::now()`.
    Step 2 — For each source `s` in `sources`, spawn a future that calls `run_g8_source(s.as_ref(), &query, G8_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE)` returning `G8SourceOutput`.
    Step 3 — Wrap the join (use `futures::future::join_all` OR `tokio::join!` per the G7 precedent) in `tokio::time::timeout(deadline, ...)`.
    Step 4 — On `Ok(results)` (master deadline NOT exceeded): build `G8Outcome { results, wall_elapsed_ms: start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64, master_timed_out: false }`.
    Step 5 — On `Err(_)` (master deadline elapsed): build `G8Outcome { results: <vec of TimedOut placeholders>, wall_elapsed_ms: deadline.as_millis() as u64, master_timed_out: true }` with one `G8SourceOutput { source_id: s.source_id(), method: s.method(), status: TimedOut, elapsed_ms: deadline.as_millis() as u64, candidates: vec![], error: Some("master deadline elapsed".into()) }` per source.
    Carries `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.testing", fields(source_count = sources.len()))]` per master-plan §15.2 lines 1518-1522 prescribed group-span name (`ucil.group.testing` — the G8 group). DO NOT add `master_deadline_ms` numeric-cast field per WO-0085 §planner lesson (`clippy::cast_possible_truncation` + `#![deny(warnings)]`).

8. **`run_g8_source` per-source helper** (private, NOT pub):
    `async fn run_g8_source(source: &(dyn G8Source + Send + Sync), query: &G8Query, deadline: Duration) -> G8SourceOutput`. Mirrors `g7.rs:347 run_g7_source` verbatim. The function:
    Step 1 — Capture `start = Instant::now()`.
    Step 2 — Wrap `source.execute(query)` in `tokio::time::timeout(deadline, ...)`.
    Step 3 — On `Ok(Ok(candidates))`: status=Available, elapsed_ms=start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64, candidates, error=None.
    Step 4 — On `Ok(Err(e))`: status=Errored, elapsed_ms=start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64, candidates=vec![], error=Some(e).
    Step 5 — On `Err(_)` (per-source timeout elapsed): status=TimedOut, elapsed_ms=deadline.as_millis() as u64, candidates=vec![], error=Some("per-source deadline elapsed".into()).
    Carries `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.g8.source", fields(source_id = source.source_id(), method = ?source.method()), skip_all)]` per master-plan §15.2 + WO-0083/WO-0085 precedent.
    **THIS is the M1 mutation target.** Per scope_in #14, the M1 form replaces `tokio::time::timeout(deadline, source.execute(query))` with the bare `source.execute(query).await` form, bypassing the per-source timeout. The literal source-line shape MUST be preserved as the M1 anchor.

9. **`join_all_g8` parallel-join helper** (private, with `#[allow(dead_code)]`):
    Per WO-0085 §executor lesson (`g7.rs:join_all_g7` precedent), introduce a small `async fn join_all_g8(futures: Vec<...>) -> Vec<G8SourceOutput>` helper that wraps `futures::future::join_all` (or equivalent `tokio` orchestration). The `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotation is REQUIRED because (a) `execute_g8` may inline the join expression OR call this helper, AND (b) M1-style mutations swapping the parallel join for a sequential `for ... .await` loop must NOT trigger `#![deny(warnings)]` for the orphaned helper. Inline rustdoc MUST cite the M1 contract (e.g., `// Helper kept #[allow(dead_code)] for M1 mutation per WO-0085 §executor lesson — sequential-await mutations swap the join callsite without orphaning this fn.`).

10. **`merge_g8_test_discoveries` merger** (pure-deterministic; with `# Examples` rustdoc):
    `pub fn merge_g8_test_discoveries(outcome: &G8Outcome) -> Vec<MergedG8TestCandidate>`. Pure-deterministic; never panics; returns sorted alphabetical-by-test_path output. Algorithm:
    Step 1 — Build a `BTreeMap<PathBuf, (HashSet<TestDiscoveryMethod>, f64, BTreeSet<PathBuf>)>` keyed by `test_path`.
    Step 2 — For each `result: &G8SourceOutput` in `outcome.results` where `status == Available`:
      For each `c: &G8TestCandidate` in `result.candidates`:
        Insert/update `(test_path, method_set, max_confidence, source_paths)` — method_set inserts `c.method`, max_confidence = max(prior, c.confidence), source_paths inserts `c.source_path` if Some.
    Step 3 — Drain into `Vec<MergedG8TestCandidate>` with `methods_found_by` sorted by enum discriminant order (Convention < Import < KgRelations); `source_paths` sorted alphabetical.
    Step 4 — Output is sorted alphabetical by `test_path` (BTreeMap iteration order).
    NO async; NO IO; NO `tokio::spawn`; NO subprocess. §15.2 tracing carve-out applies (pure-deterministic projection over `outcome` already in memory) per WO-0067 §planner #2 + WO-0084 §planner pure-deterministic carve-out + WO-0085 §planner pure-deterministic carve-out for `merge_g7_by_severity`. Document the carve-out inline.
    **THIS is the M2 mutation target.** Per scope_in #14, the M2 form changes the dedup key from `test_path` to `(test_path, method)` — every (test_path, method) pair becomes its own MergedG8TestCandidate, preventing the union-by-method aggregation.

11. **Frozen test `test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods`** at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`) per DEC-0007 + WO-0070/0083/0085 precedent. Write as:
    ```rust
    #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")]
    pub async fn test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods() { /* ... */ }
    ```
    Use `multi_thread` flavor (NOT default `current_thread`) so the parallelism wall-clock guard SA6 actually exercises `tokio::join!` parallel scheduling — current_thread would serialise the spawns and trip SA6 falsely. Match the WO-0083 `test_g4_architecture_query` flavor (`#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")]`).
    Pre-flight: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods --list 2>&1 | grep -c 'test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods'` returns exactly 1.

12. **Frozen test body** — construct three `TestG8Source` impls under `#[cfg(test)]` and one `TestG8Source` variant per `TestDiscoveryMethod`:
    ```rust
    #[cfg(test)]
    struct TestG8Source { id: String, method: TestDiscoveryMethod, behavior: TestG8Behavior }
    #[cfg(test)]
    enum TestG8Behavior {
        Available { candidates: Vec<G8TestCandidate>, sleep_ms: u64 },
        Slow { sleep_ms: u64 }, // sleeps past G8_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE → TimedOut
        Errored { msg: String },
    }
    ```
    Construct three sources:
      - `convention`: id="convention-1", method=Convention, Available with candidates=[G8TestCandidate { test_path: tests/test_foo.rs, source_path: Some(src/foo.rs), method: Convention, confidence: 0.9 }, G8TestCandidate { test_path: tests/test_shared.rs, source_path: Some(src/shared.rs), method: Convention, confidence: 0.8 }], sleep_ms=10.
      - `import`: id="import-1", method=Import, Available with candidates=[G8TestCandidate { test_path: tests/test_shared.rs, source_path: Some(src/foo.rs), method: Import, confidence: 0.7 }], sleep_ms=10. (NOTE: tests/test_shared.rs is ALSO discovered by Convention — the merger MUST union them.)
      - `kg`: id="kg-1", method=KgRelations, Available with candidates=[G8TestCandidate { test_path: tests/test_bar.rs, source_path: Some(src/bar.rs), method: KgRelations, confidence: 0.6 }], sleep_ms=10.
    Call `execute_g8(query, sources, G8_DEFAULT_MASTER_DEADLINE).await` and assert the SA-tagged invariants in scope_in #13.

13. **Frozen test SA assertions** (each with operator-readable panic body per WO-0085 §verifier `(SAn) <name>; left: ..., right: ...` pattern):
    (SA1) `outcome.results.len() == 3` (one per source). Panic body: `(SA1) results.len() expected 3; observed {n}`.
    (SA2) `outcome.master_timed_out == false` (master deadline does NOT trip in the all-Available scenario). Panic body: `(SA2) master_timed_out expected false; observed {b}`.
    (SA3) Each source's `status` matches its declared behavior — convention/import/kg all `Available`. Panic body per source: `(SA3.<id>) status expected Available; observed {s:?}`.
    (SA4) **M1-targeted**: Add a 4th source `slow`: id="slow-1", method=Convention, behavior=Slow{sleep_ms=5500} (sleeps 5500ms — exceeds G8_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE 4500ms but NOT master 5000ms... actually exceeds master too; rebalance: `slow` sleeps 5500ms with G8_DEFAULT_MASTER_DEADLINE bumped to 6500ms IN THE TEST CALL only by passing `Duration::from_millis(6_500)` as the `deadline` argument). Assert `outcome.results[3].status == G8SourceStatus::TimedOut`. Panic body: `(SA4) outcome.results[3].status; left: TimedOut, right: {s:?}`. **This assertion is targeted by M1 (per-source timeout bypass): if M1 removes the per-source `tokio::time::timeout(G8_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE, ...)` wrapper, the slow source completes after 5500ms instead of timing out at 4500ms — but since master is 6500ms, the slow source returns Available, flipping SA4.**
    (SA5) **M2-targeted**: Apply `merge_g8_test_discoveries(&outcome)` and assert that `tests/test_shared.rs` appears EXACTLY ONCE in the merged output (NOT twice — even though it was discovered by both Convention and Import). Assert `merged.iter().filter(|m| m.test_path == PathBuf::from("tests/test_shared.rs")).count() == 1` AND `merged.iter().find(|m| m.test_path == PathBuf::from("tests/test_shared.rs")).unwrap().methods_found_by == vec![TestDiscoveryMethod::Convention, TestDiscoveryMethod::Import]` (sorted by enum discriminant). Panic body: `(SA5a) tests/test_shared.rs dedup count expected 1; observed {n}`. AND `(SA5b) methods_found_by expected [Convention, Import]; observed {v:?}`. **This assertion is targeted by M2 (dedup-key inversion): if M2 changes the merger key from `test_path` to `(test_path, method)`, tests/test_shared.rs appears TWICE (once per method), flipping SA5a.**
    (SA6) **Parallelism wall-clock backstop**: `outcome.wall_elapsed_ms < 5_000` (sequential execution of 3+1=4 sources with 10ms+10ms+10ms+5500ms-but-bounded-to-4500ms-by-per-source-timeout = ~4500-5000ms wall; parallel execution = max(10ms, 10ms, 10ms, 4500ms) = ~4500ms). Note: the wall is dominated by the slow source's per-source timeout (4500ms). For SA6 to be load-bearing on parallelism: in the absence of the slow source the wall would be ~10ms parallel vs ~30ms sequential — but with the slow source it's ~4500ms either way. **Re-cast SA6 as: `outcome.wall_elapsed_ms < 5_000` (within master deadline), confirming master deadline NOT exceeded** — this is a backstop against `master_timed_out=true` regression. Panic body: `(SA6) wall_elapsed_ms within master deadline expected <5000; observed {n}`.
    (SA7) **Errored source preserves Error status**: replace one of the 3 method sources OR add a 5th with `behavior: TestG8Behavior::Errored { msg: "intentional test error".into() }`. Assert `result.status == Errored && result.error == Some("intentional test error".into())`. Panic body: `(SA7) errored source status expected Errored with msg; observed status={s:?} error={e:?}`.
    (SA8) **Sentinel-row vocabulary canary** for `TestDiscoveryMethod`: assert that serializing a `TestDiscoveryMethod` value via `serde_json::to_string` produces snake_case lowercase strings: `serde_json::to_string(&TestDiscoveryMethod::Convention).unwrap() == "\"convention\""` AND similar for `Import`/`KgRelations` (kg_relations). Use `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]` on the enum to enforce this serialisation contract. Panic body per variant: `(SA8.<variant>) snake_case serialisation expected "<expected>"; observed {s}`. This is the §3.2/§5.8-vocabulary regression canary — analogous to the WO-0067 §6.2 sentinel-row + WO-0085 §5.7 severity sentinel-row patterns.
    Test MUST run via `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")]`; MUST NOT touch the network; uses only `std::path::PathBuf` + `std::time::Duration` + the local `TestG8Source` helpers.

14. **Pre-bake 2 mutation entries** — pattern carried from WO-0042 → WO-0088 (now 44+ WOs deep). Each mutation: in-place `Edit` patch + targeted cargo-test selector + `git checkout --` restore + md5sum verify.
    **M1 (per-source timeout bypass)**: Edit `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs` to bypass the per-source `tokio::time::timeout` wrapper. Find the line containing `tokio::time::timeout(deadline, source.execute(query))` (or equivalent in `run_g8_source`) and change it to `source.execute(query).await`. The structural shape: replace `match tokio::time::timeout(deadline, source.execute(query)).await { Ok(Ok(c)) => ..., Ok(Err(e)) => ..., Err(_) => ... }` with `match source.execute(query).await { Ok(c) => ..., Err(e) => ... }` — eliminates the TimedOut arm. Re-run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods`; MUST FAIL with the SA4 panic (`(SA4) outcome.results[3].status; left: TimedOut, right: Available`) — the slow source now returns Available instead of TimedOut. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs`. Pre-md5: `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs > /tmp/wo-0089-g8-orig.md5`.
    **M2 (merger dedup-key inversion)**: Edit `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs` to invert the merger dedup key. Find `BTreeMap<PathBuf, ...>` keyed by `test_path` in `merge_g8_test_discoveries` and change to `BTreeMap<(PathBuf, TestDiscoveryMethod), ...>` keyed by `(test_path, method)`. The structural shape: every (test_path, method) pair becomes its own MergedG8TestCandidate, breaking the union-by-method aggregation. Re-run; MUST FAIL with SA5a (`(SA5a) tests/test_shared.rs dedup count expected 1; observed 2`) — tests/test_shared.rs now appears TWICE (once per method). Restore: `git checkout --`.
    Each mutation ≤ 5 lines of source change, ≤ 60 seconds wall-time. Pre-stage ONE md5 snapshot (M1+M2 both target g8.rs): `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs > /tmp/wo-0089-g8-orig.md5` BEFORE applying any mutation; confirm restoration via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0089-g8-orig.md5` after each `git checkout --`.
    Spirit-over-literal carve-out per WO-0070/0083/0084/0086/0088 §planner: the executor MAY tighten the targeted SA OR select an alternate-form mutation if the prescribed form (a) compiles but doesn't trip the prescribed SA cleanly, OR (b) the same mutation also flips an earlier SA in assertion order. Document any deviation in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section.

15. **Tracing instrumentation per master-plan §15.2 lines 1518-1522**:
    `execute_g8` carries `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.testing", fields(source_count = sources.len()))]`.
    `run_g8_source` carries `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.g8.source", fields(source_id = source.source_id(), method = ?source.method()), skip_all)]`.
    `merge_g8_test_discoveries` is pure-deterministic — NO `#[tracing::instrument]` (carve-out per WO-0067 §planner #2 + WO-0084 §planner + WO-0085 §planner). Document the carve-out inline in the merger's rustdoc.
    NO `master_deadline_ms` numeric-cast field per WO-0085 §planner lesson (`clippy::cast_possible_truncation` + `#![deny(warnings)]`); auto-capture via fn-arg only.

16. **`lib.rs` updates**:
    Add `pub mod g8;` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` in alphabetical position. Pre-flight: `grep -E '^pub mod' crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` shows the existing block (e.g., `pub mod branch_manager; pub mod executor; pub mod g2_search; pub mod g3; pub mod g4; pub mod g7; ...`). Add `pub mod g8;` between `pub mod g7;` and the next alphabetical entry. NO logic in `lib.rs` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Crate-layout.
    Pre-flight post-edit: `grep -qE '^pub mod g8;' crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` returns success.

17. **No new Cargo dependency.** `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` MUST NOT change under this WO. Pre-flight: `git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml | wc -l` returns 0. The workspace's existing `tokio`, `serde`, `serde_json`, `tracing`, `futures` deps are sufficient (verified at planner-time via `grep -E '^(tokio|serde|serde_json|tracing|futures)' crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml`).

18. **Errors**: per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Errors — the `G8Source::execute` trait method returns `Result<Vec<G8TestCandidate>, String>` (NOT a typed `Error` enum). Production impls' richer error contracts will be wrapped/converted to `String` at the trait boundary — same shape as `G7Source::execute` (`g7.rs:322-340`). NO `thiserror` enum required for the trait surface. NO `anyhow` (binary-only). NO `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Errors. If the executor finds an unavoidable `.unwrap()` pattern in production code (e.g., to anchor a mutation contract per WO-0085 W2), proactively cite in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section + add a `# Panics` rustdoc proving unreachability.

19. **No `unsafe` blocks. No `#![allow(...)]` at the file or function level (clippy-clean expected).** EXCEPTION: the `join_all_g8` private helper carries pinpoint `#[allow(dead_code)]` per scope_in #9 + WO-0085 §executor lesson. NO other allows. NO `pub use` re-exports beyond the named scope_in #16 surface (NO leaking of internal helpers like `run_g8_source` or `join_all_g8` into the public API).

20. **§11 hot-cold tier — DOES NOT APPLY** to this WO (the trait + parallel orchestrator + merger are pure-projection over in-memory structures; no cache surfaces; no warm-tier promotion). Future ADR may explore caching `G8Outcome` across queries when daemon-side production-wiring lands.

21. **Mutation-contract integrity**: when applying M1 OR M2, the mutation MUST surface the regression via the cargo-test panic body (the SA-tagged failure mode named in scope_in #13). Mutations that produce a compile error OR pass the test silently are NOT acceptable — alternate mutation forms targeting the same load-bearing line ARE acceptable per WO-0070/0083/0084/0086/0088 spirit-over-literal precedent, documented in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section. Per WO-0086 §planner: PREFER constant-flip / value-change patches over comment-out patches whenever the source language permits.

22. **AC commit-cadence aligned to scope_in actual count per WO-0086 §planner**: this WO is a 1-feature G-backbone in 1 NEW source file + 1 lib.rs edit + 1 executor.rs extension. Expected 4-6 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence: (1) `feat(daemon): scaffold g8 module with G8Source trait + types + constants`; (2) `feat(daemon): implement execute_g8 parallel orchestrator + run_g8_source helper`; (3) `feat(daemon): implement merge_g8_test_discoveries pure-deterministic dedup-by-test-path merger`; (4) `test(daemon): executor::test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods frozen test with 3 TestG8Source impls + 8 SA assertions`; (5) `chore(daemon): wire g8 module through lib.rs`; (6) `docs(work-orders): WO-0089 ready-for-review`. Commits (1)+(2)+(3) MAY be folded per DEC-0005 module-coherence. Either shape is acceptable. AC30 reads 'feat branch carries 4-7 non-merge commits' to absorb the variance.

23. **Carry-forward standing protocol** (44+ WOs deep, WO-0042..WO-0088): coverage-gate.sh sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround — `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the authoritative measurement. Skip the gate-script `coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 80 75` `line=0%` exit per AC25 standing protocol. Pre-existing standing scope_outs: AC30/AC31 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake (3 open escalations); `scripts/reality-check.sh` pre-existing-stash bug; 'no merge commits introduced on feat' wording.

24. **Verifier dispatch instructions**: MUST run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods` from a clean session (`cargo clean` recommended for the verifier's first pass; not strictly required since the WO is additive in `ucil-daemon` only). MUST NOT set any `UCIL_SKIP_*` env-var (this WO has no skip gate; the test is fully deterministic, ≤6s wall-time bounded by the slow-source per-source timeout 4500ms + overhead). Per WO-0086 §verifier 'Pre-flight check' the `which cargo rustc` is sufficient — no node/npx/python/docker needed.

25. **RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include**: (a) summary; (b) M1/M2 mutation contracts in the canonical six-row shape (file, patch, selector, expected SA panic, restore, pre-md5); (c) test-run output for `test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods` (PASS line + duration); (d) any disclosed deviations from scope_in (per WO-0070/0083/0084/0086/0088 spirit-over-literal pattern); (e) coverage diagnostic (`env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only` line %); (f) pre-md5 snapshot path confirmed restored via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0089-g8-orig.md5` returning OK lines. Mirror the WO-0085 / WO-0088 RFR Mutation contract section verbatim in shape.

26. **Bundle-shape rationale** (cite in commit body for the first feat commit): single-feature G-backbone WO per WO-0086 §planner explicit recommendation. P3-W11-F09 has no peer features in the feature-list sharing the G8 thematic invariant — F10 + F14 depend on F09 transitively. The G-backbone template is SEXTUPLE-validated (WO-0047 G1, WO-0070 G3, WO-0083 G4, WO-0085 G7); single-feature WOs are precedent-supported (WO-0079/0080/0087). Estimated 4-6 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence + WO-0086 §planner AC-cadence-alignment lesson.
27. **AC #19 'no merge commits introduced on feat' wording** per WO-0070 §planner #4: `git log feat/WO-0089-g8-test-discovery-backbone ^main --merges` returns empty (NOT the 'feat..main = 0' form).

28. **Word-ban grep** MUST cover only production-side files. The grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` returns empty before first push. Per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 §executor carve-out, the test body's panic strings (e.g., `(SA1) ...`) and the test-side `TestG8Source` helpers in `executor.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` mod are NOT subject to word-ban scrutiny. The `Test` prefix on `TestG8Source` is NOT in the ban list (WO-0083 / WO-0085 precedent).

29. **Frozen test placement** verified PRE-PUSH: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods --list 2>&1 | grep -cE 'test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods'` shows exactly 1 match. NO `mod tests { ... }` wrapper around the frozen test (per scope_in #11).

30. **`# Panics` rustdoc requirement** for any `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` shipped in production code (per WO-0085 §planner W2 + RFR-disclosure rule). The G8 backbone is designed to never panic — error chains via `Result<T, String>`, no array indexing, no Option-unwrapping in production code. If the executor finds an unavoidable `.unwrap()` (e.g., to anchor a mutation contract), the unwrap MUST carry a `# Panics` rustdoc proving unreachability AND the executor MUST cite in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section per WO-0085 lesson.

31. **Master-plan §6.1 + §5.8 alignment**: `execute_g8` per-group timeout 5000ms matches §6.1 line 606 'Per-group timeout: 5s default'. The 3 discovery methods (Convention, Import, KgRelations) match §5.8 lines 572-574 verbatim. The `ucil.group.testing` tracing span name is the §15.2 prescribed group-span (the 8 group spans are ucil.group.{lookup, search, dependency, architecture, context, platform, quality, testing}). Future production-wiring will add a 4th method (Coverage-based per §5.8 line 575) — that's a separate feature OR scope-extension WO.

32. **Async-trait shape decision**: INSPECT `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs:322-340` G7Source signature BEFORE writing G8Source. If G7Source uses `async fn execute(...)` directly (Rust 2024 edition's stable in-trait async-fn), MIRROR. If G7Source uses `Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = ...> + Send + '_>>` return type, MIRROR. Use whichever is the project's established convention. The `Cargo.toml` `[package].edition` field for `ucil-daemon` confirms which form is supported. NO `async-trait` proc-macro dependency — the workspace's existing form is canonical.

33. **Test-corpus realism**: the test path strings (`tests/test_foo.rs`, `tests/test_shared.rs`, `tests/test_bar.rs`, `src/foo.rs`, `src/shared.rs`, `src/bar.rs`) are LITERAL strings used in the test body — NOT file-system paths that need to exist on disk. The `TestG8Source` impls construct `G8TestCandidate` literal values; no `tempfile::TempDir` required. Production impls wired in a future WO will read real corpus paths via `tokio::fs` / `walkdir` / KG queries, but the trait surface is corpus-agnostic.

34. **Slow source's behavior within master deadline**: SA4 (M1-target) requires the test call `execute_g8(query, sources, deadline)` with a `deadline` argument that EXCEEDS the slow source's sleep_ms (e.g., `deadline = Duration::from_millis(6_500)` and `slow.sleep_ms = 5_500` — slow exceeds G8_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE 4500ms but NOT master 6500ms). This way the slow source's per-source-timeout fires (TimedOut) BUT the master deadline does NOT (master_timed_out=false, SA2). Without this rebalance, the master deadline would also trip on the slow source, conflating M1 with the master-deadline path.
scope_out: 1. Do NOT wire `execute_g8` / `merge_g8_test_discoveries` into the daemon's MCP-tool dispatch path OR into any `server::*` cargo selector — those wirings are the consumer-WO scope (P3-W11-F10 `check_quality` MCP tool / P3-W11-F11 `review_changes` MCP tool / P3-W11-F14 testing pipeline integration test).

2. Do NOT introduce production `G8Source` impls (`ConventionG8Source` walking fixture conventions, `ImportG8Source` parsing imports, `KgRelationsG8Source` querying KG `tested_by` relations). Those are deferred to a follow-up production-wiring WO that bundles daemon-startup orchestration — same shape as the G3/G4/G7 production-wiring deferrals (WO-0083 / WO-0085 / WO-0086 §planner). The trait + 3 `TestG8Source` impls in `#[cfg(test)]` are the deliverable.

3. Do NOT introduce a 4th method (Coverage-based per master-plan §5.8 line 575). Coverage-based test discovery requires execution-time coverage data (which test files historically cover the changed lines) — that's a separate WO scope-extension OR a future feature in the feature-list. The 3 methods (Convention, Import, KgRelations) per F09 description are the deliverable.
4. Do NOT modify the cross-group fusion contract OR `CrossGroupFusedOutcome` shape — G8 outputs are downstream-of-fusion, not within-fusion. NO `crates/ucil-core/src/cross_group.rs` edits.

5. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g3.rs`, `g4.rs`, `g7.rs`, `g2_search.rs`, `executor.rs` (beyond the additive `test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods` test fn + helpers + `#[cfg(test)] use crate::g8::*;`), `server.rs`, `lifecycle.rs`, `plugin_manager.rs`, `branch_manager.rs`, `lancedb_indexer.rs`, `priority_queue.rs`, `scip.rs`, `session_manager.rs`, `session_ttl.rs`, `startup.rs`, `storage.rs`, `text_search.rs`, `understand_code.rs`, `watcher.rs`, `test_support.rs` — all out-of-scope (forbidden_paths covers each EXCEPT `executor.rs` which is allow-listed for the additive test extension).

6. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**` — F09 is a daemon-side parallel-orchestrator backbone, NOT a core-library extension.

7. Do NOT alter `scripts/gate/**` or `scripts/flip-feature.sh` (forbidden_paths). NO new `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F09.sh` is needed — F09's acceptance test is a `cargo_test` (not a script) per `feature-list.json`.

8. Do NOT add new env-vars. The `G8_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE` / `G8_DEFAULT_MASTER_DEADLINE` constants are compile-time per scope_in #4; runtime tuning lives in the trait surface (callers pass `deadline: Duration` to `execute_g8`).
9. Do NOT introduce `unsafe` blocks. The hand-rolled parallel-orchestrator + merger are safe Rust over `Vec<Box<dyn G8Source>>` + `BTreeMap<PathBuf, ...>` — no FFI, no transmute, no raw pointers.
10. Do NOT split the WO-0089 deliverable across multiple feat-branches. ONE feat branch (`feat/WO-0089-g8-test-discovery-backbone`) per DEC-0005 module-coherence.
11. Do NOT add `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, OR `xfail` to the frozen test. Fully deterministic and bounded ≤6s wall-time on the in-memory inputs. Per .claude/CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract.

12. Do NOT add backwards-compat shims for the public `G8Source` / `G8Query` / `G8Outcome` / `MergedG8TestCandidate` types — this is the FIRST landing of these types, no consumers exist yet, no compat surface to preserve.

13. Do NOT introduce a separate benchmark file under `crates/ucil-daemon/benches/`. Benchmark coverage for the G8 backbone is OUT OF SCOPE (a future ADR may add a `criterion` bench when daemon-side production-wiring lands and the parallel-orchestration shape becomes performance-critical).
14. Do NOT introduce or modify any LLM-provider, embedding-provider, OR network-touching surface. The G8 backbone runs locally + deterministically on in-memory `G8Query` payloads.
15. Do NOT introduce graph-DB or external-service sidecar provisioning. The trait-with-test-impl-only deliverable operates over in-memory inputs.
16. Do NOT modify scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh OR scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh — the standing carry-forwards (44+ WOs deep) remain out-of-scope.
17. Verifier MUST NOT set any `UCIL_SKIP_*` envs (this WO has no skip gate; the test is purely deterministic).
18. Do NOT add new entries to `feature-list.json` — verifier flips `passes` via `scripts/flip-feature.sh`.
19. Do NOT modify any plugin manifests under `plugins/**` — F09 is a daemon-side orchestrator, NOT a plugin manifest.

20. Do NOT modify any tests under `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/**` — the WO-0089 unit test lives at MODULE ROOT of `executor.rs` (NOT as integration tests). The cargo selector requires module-root placement per scope_in #11.

21. Do NOT introduce additional `#[cfg(test)]` helper structs beyond the named `TestG8Source` (scope_in #12). NO `MockG8Source` / `FakeG8Source` / `StubG8Source` — those names are word-ban-violating per scope_in #28.
22. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]` OR `[dev-dependencies]`. Pre-flight: `git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml | wc -l` returns 0.

23. Do NOT add `serde::Deserialize` / `Serialize` to types beyond what the wire-shape contract requires (`G8SourceStatus`, `G8SourceOutput`, `G8Outcome`, `TestDiscoveryMethod`, `MergedG8TestCandidate` — the types that flow over MCP tool boundaries in future production WOs). The `G8Source` trait + `G8TestCandidate` data struct + `G8Query` MAY also derive `Serialize/Deserialize` for symmetry but are not strictly required at this WO's deliverable scope.

24. Do NOT introduce trait objects (`Arc<dyn G8Source>`) in the public API beyond the `Vec<Box<dyn G8Source + Send + Sync + 'static>>` argument to `execute_g8`. Consumer-WO concern (when daemon-side multi-source orchestration lands) — same shape as `Vec<Box<dyn G7Source>>` (`g7.rs`).
